feed.title: SAP Community - PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance
feed.subtitle: PLM EAM/Plant Maintenance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Impact on Resource Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know if we remove MRS config will it have any impact on RSH.

Thanks & Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Impact on RSH
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know if we remove only configuration from the path SAP MultiResource Scheduling - Sources of Demand - PM/CS Orders - Define Resource Planning Relevance for PM/CS Orders related to MRS then will it have any impact on RSH

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Unexpected Gap in Order Number Sequence
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have identified an issue in the system regarding order number sequencing.

An order was created with the number 4400210, and the next order generated has the number 4408779. This indicates that more than 8,500 order numbers were skipped.

We are unsure why this gap occurred. Could you please help us understand the reason behind this behavior?
Is this expected system behavior, or does it indicate a configuration or technical issue?

Thank you in advance for your support.

 

Best regards,

entry.title: External Resource Setup in SAP RSH
entry.content: We are starting SAP RSH implementation project on S/4HANA 2023 SP01 on-premise system. We plan to manage our routine maintenance activities on SAP RSH and assign the work order operations to named persons. We use a number of contractors (external resources) to conduct the maintenance work. These contractors are not created as employees in our  HR system or have SAP user ID. What is the best way to setup these resources in S/4HANA system with out adding them to HR as an employee? Is there anyway, we can just create Business Partners (like BP with BBP005 role) and use them for resource assignment?

entry.title: Activación de vista de costos en aplicativo Gestión de órdenes de mantenimiento F5241
entry.content:
Buena tarde, estoy tratando de solucionar un inconveniente en el aplicativo " Gestión de órdenes de mantenimiento  ID  F5241", el cual no me muestra los costos aplicados en la orden por mano de obra, materiales y servicios.

En estos momentos en ese Tab esta deshabilitada la función y no se si hay que hacer algo en el sistema para que me muestre los costos de la orden PM.

entry.title: Activating the Cost View in the F5241 Maintenance Order Management Application
entry.content:
Good afternoon, I'm trying to resolve an issue with the "Maintenance Order Management ID F5241" application, which isn't displaying the costs applied to the order for labor, materials, and services.

Currently, the function is disabled in that tab, and I'm not sure if I need to do something in the system to enable it to display the PM order costs.

entry.title: Impact on the processes
entry.content:
Hi Team,

If we do below configuration. What would be the impact:

  1. Define the plan categories for estimated costs, planned costs and baseline costs in Customizing of Controlling under General Controlling > Planning > Maintain Category for Planning. Define the three plan categories (e.g. PMORDER01, PMORDER02, PMORDER03) for the application type Maintenance Order and with No specific usage. Do not allow import, copy or delete.

  2. Define which of the three plan categories that you defined in the previous step is valid for estimated costs, which one for planned costs and which one for baseline costs. You can do this in Customizing for Controlling under General Controlling > Planning > Application-Specific Categories > Maintain Categories for Maintenance Orders. Note that you cannot change this assignment retrospectively.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Mass download attached documents linked in Maintenance orders in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Good day! 

Is there a way for us to mass download attached documents in maintenance orders? 

or if anyone has developed a custom API to mass download?

Thank you

Regards, 

Alicia

entry.title: Equipment Installation and Dismantle app
entry.content:
Hi All , 

Greetings !

In our current system , system is generating maintenance notification and posting material automatically whenever we install or dismantle any equipment using fiori app "Equipment Installation and Dismantle " or IE4N t-code . How can we prevent auto generation of such notification & auto goods movement ? How we can track all the goods movement and all the history for an equipment ? 

Thanking you .

Mohammed

entry.title: PM-Revisions not seen in OIOB
entry.content:
I created a revision using OIOB t-code and I can see the entry in the table, T352R.

However, I cant see the same entry when I go back to OIOB. There are no entries there. 

How do I resolve this?

Thanks and Regards,

Anirudh

entry.title: IH01 – Plant Field Is Prefilled by Default
entry.content:
Hello,

Every time I enter transaction code IH01, the Plant field is prefilled. How can I make this field come up empty for all users?
If a variant has been created, how was this done without entering the FL field? I have seen solutions related to creating a variant with SAP_TCODE_IH01; however, in that case the FL field needs to be filled in as well.

SU3/Parameters

entry.title: SAP ECC 6.0 version: Hierachical strategy
entry.content:
Hello!

How is it hierachical strategy creation in ECC SAP (ECC 6 version). If create hierachical strategy for example and create preventive maintenance plan and select packages 1 WEEK and 3 WEEK. Question: Is is so that on 2 WEEK there is NO any calls?

1 WEEK  H1

2 WEEK  H2

3 WEEK H3

entry.title: Follow on order
entry.content:
Hi All ,

Greetings , 

We are creating follow on order from change maintenance order fiori app  but I am not able find linkage between main order and follow on order . I am checking in document flow but could not find . Can anyone please shed some light where to find the linkage ? We are in SAP S4 Hana Private cloud 2023 . 

Thanking you .

Mohammed

entry.title: Unexpected Object Lists in Maintenance Orders Generated by IP30 from Mce Plans
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help with an issue related to maintenance plans.

I have maintenance plans that are scheduled daily via job IP30. Some of the maintenance orders created contain object lists (two functional locations). However, I cannot identify where these object lists are coming from, as the maintenance plan itself does not contain any object list.

Do you have any idea where these object lists could be inherited from?
Also, which configuration or master data should I analyze to investigate this behavior?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP EAM Fiori Skip Dispatch phase without Resource Scheduling
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

We are using SAP PM with Phase Based Maintenance Process in Fiori. We do not have license for Resource Scheduling and therefore would like to skip Dispatch Phase, so the idea is to go from REL to Perform Maintenance Job directly without the need of making the extra step of dispatching the Order. Without Resource Scheduling it seems quite useless to be honest...

Is it possible to skip by changing customizing in Define Subphases and Phases? Or this is somehow hardcode and not possible to skip? Any BADI we can influence?

Also I think Phase Control is useful to block jumping from one phase to other phase, but as long as I know no chance to skip phases.

Thanks and Kind Regards

entry.title: Impact on RSH to remove the below config setting for the node related to MRS
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are planning to remove the config setting for the node SAP MultiResource Scheduling - Sources of Demand - PM/CS Orders - Define Resource Planning Relevance for PM/CS Orders. As we were unable to release the work orders with the personnel number assigned as those personnel numbers are not available in MRS table - /MRSS/D_BAS_RES  . Will this have any impact on RSH?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Impact on RSH after deactivating MRS Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you please let me know deactivation of which MRS configuration node will impact RSH?

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: PM Labor Cost Reporting
entry.content:
Hi all,

In all the reporting screens I have found—including IW38, IW47, IW37N, S_ALR_87012993, MCI8, KKBC_ORD, KOC4, and KOB1. Material costs and labor costs are shown as a single total on an order level. Is there a report screen where I can extract these two separately by order, or one where I can pull only the labor costs?

entry.title: Returned Quantity Field Does Not Allow Decimal Values in Perform Maintenance Job App in FIORI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Please I have a question, In the Fiori app Perform Maintenance Job, the dialog Return Goods does not allow the entry of decimal values in the Returned Quantity field when returning components from a maintenance order.

The input field only accepts integer values, even for materials whose unit of measure supports decimal quantities.

 

 

Please someone have any solution?

Thank you,

Regards

entry.title: Situation Handling for Maintenance Order – Trigger Notification at Post-Execution Phase in SAP Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am working with Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Asset Management / EAM) and have a requirement to notify Production users when a maintenance order reaches the post-execution stage (i.e., maintenance work is completed and production can resume).

I explored the standard situation type
EAM_MAINT_ORDER_PHASE_CHANGED (Maintenance Order Phase Changed).

However, while configuring the conditions / anchor object filters, I noticed that:

  • Order Phase (Execution / Post-Execution) is not available as a condition

  • System Status (CNF / TECO) is also not available as a filter

  • Only header attributes such as Plant, Order Type, Equipment, Dates, etc. are exposed

In contrast, for Maintenance Notifications, phase and subphase are available as conditions, making it easier to trigger notifications.

My questions are:

  1. How can we trigger a situation notification specifically when a maintenance order enters the post-execution stage in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

  2. Is there any standard workaround or recommended approach to achieve this using Situation Handling?

  3. Is this a known limitation by design for maintenance orders in Public Cloud?

The goal is purely to inform Production users (via Fiori notification or email) that the maintenance activity is completed and they can resume operations — without using custom developments, as this is a Public Cloud system.

Any guidance, best practices, or confirmation from SAP experts would be greatly appreciated.


Regards,
Sriram R
